operating_state: Net sales declined 30% to $8.5 billion driven by broad-based equipment weakness with dealer inventory destocking and reduced customer demand across U.S., Canada, and Europe. Financial services revenues remained flat at $1.6 billion despite lower receivable balances due to higher financing rates. Operating profit fell 64% to $793 million as lower volumes and unfavorable mix more than offset cost reduction efforts.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio deteriorated to 74.0% from 68.7% primarily due to overhead absorption inefficiencies from reduced production volumes, partially offset by lower material costs. Operating margins compressed significantly across all equipment segments with production and precision agriculture falling to 11.0% from 21.6%. Operating expenses decreased reflecting ongoing cost management initiatives and reduced employee profit-sharing incentives.
material_events: Completed Banco John Deere joint venture with Bradesco retaining 50% equity interest and recording $32 million pretax gain from valuation allowance reversal. Issued $2 billion in long-term debt through Notes due 2035 and 2055 in January 2025. Made voluntary $520 million contribution to U.S. OPEB plan, declared quarterly dividend of $1.62 per share, and repurchased $441 million of common stock during the quarter.
macro_exposures: Federal Trade Commission and multiple state attorneys general filed antitrust lawsuit in January 2025 alleging monopolization in equipment repair services market, seeking permanent injunction for independent repair access. Foreign exchange headwinds intensified with cumulative translation adjustments of negative $451 million reflecting broad-based currency weakness against the dollar. Uncertainty regarding potential import tariffs and retaliatory actions could affect outlook in rapidly evolving trade environment.
forward_outlook: Management expects sales volumes to decline in 2025 due to reduced demand across agriculture, turf, and construction markets with agriculture and turf demand expected down in North America and Europe, flat in South America. Construction equipment sales projected down in North America with flat global forestry and roadbuilding markets. Financial services net income expected up with favorable provision trends but unfavorable financing spreads.
uncertainty_profile: Agricultural market business cycle effects expected to continue with lower sales volumes, higher sales incentives, and elevated receivable write-offs. Interest rates remain elevated despite recent decreases, negatively impacting product demand and financing spreads. Right to repair regulations represent ongoing regulatory uncertainty alongside pending accounting standard changes.
